# UC-18.3.22 ¬∑ VXLAN Underlay Path MTU and DF Bit Fragmentation Risk

## Description

VXLAN adds overhead; MTU mismatches or PMTUD black holes cause silent throughput collapse and TCP retransmits on east-west paths. Tracking DF-bit probes and ICMP unreachables across the underlay separates fabric issues from guest OS misconfiguration before tickets flood application teams.

## Value

VXLAN adds overhead; MTU mismatches or PMTUD black holes cause silent throughput collapse and TCP retransmits on east-west paths. Tracking DF-bit probes and ICMP unreachables across the underlay separates fabric issues from guest OS misconfiguration before tickets flood application teams.

## Implementation

(1) Run scheduled jumbo ping/UDP probes between loopbacks with DF set. (2) Ingest syslog `ICMP unreachable` / `MTU` messages. (3) Document expected MTU (for example 9216) per site and alert on regression.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-cisco_ios`, `arista:eos` via SC4S, ICMP probe scripted input.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype="fabric:mtu_diag"` with fields `src_leaf`, `dst_leaf`, `max_mtu_ok`, `icmp_needfrag`, `test_size`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Run scheduled jumbo ping/UDP probes between loopbacks with DF set. (2) Ingest syslog `ICMP unreachable` / `MTU` messages. (3) Document expected MTU (for example 9216) per site and alert on regression.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="fabric:mtu_diag" earliest=-24h
| where max_mtu_ok=0 OR icmp_needfrag>0
| stats sum(icmp_needfrag) as needfrag max(test_size) as last_size by src_leaf, dst_leaf
| sort -needfrag
| table src_leaf, dst_leaf, needfrag, last_size
```

Understanding this SPL

**VXLAN Underlay Path MTU and DF Bit Fragmentation Risk** ‚Äî VXLAN adds overhead; MTU mismatches or PMTUD black holes cause silent throughput collapse and TCP retransmits on east-west paths. Tracking DF-bit probes and ICMP unreachables across the underlay separates fabric issues from guest OS misconfiguration before tickets flood application teams.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype="fabric:mtu_diag"` with fields `src_leaf`, `dst_leaf`, `max_mtu_ok`, `icmp_needfrag`, `test_size`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-cisco_ios`, `arista:eos` via SC4S, ICMP probe scripted input. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: fabric:mtu_diag. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="fabric:mtu_diag", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where max_mtu_ok=0 OR icmp_needfrag>0` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_leaf, dst_leaf** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **VXLAN Underlay Path MTU and DF Bit Fragmentation Risk**): table src_leaf, dst_leaf, needfrag, last_size


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm results in the index, then compare with the same scope in the relevant fabric/SDN controller or manager UI.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (bad pairs), Diagram (site √ó path status), Single value (paths failing MTU).

## SPL

```spl
index=network sourcetype="fabric:mtu_diag" earliest=-24h
| where max_mtu_ok=0 OR icmp_needfrag>0
| stats sum(icmp_needfrag) as needfrag max(test_size) as last_size by src_leaf, dst_leaf
| sort -needfrag
| table src_leaf, dst_leaf, needfrag, last_size
```

## Visualization

Table (bad pairs), Diagram (site √ó path status), Single value (paths failing MTU).
